properties.name: Maharac Cape
properties.cargo: amphorae; Eastern coarse ware pottery of biconical dishes and trefoil mouth jugs.

properties.name: Veliki Skolj
properties.cargo: marble; sarcophagi; marble:15 basins or unfinished sarcophagi with lids.; Sherds of Eastern coarse ware two-handled dishes, oinochoe.

properties.name: Kizil Burun Column

properties.name: Vele Orjule/Lussino
properties.cargo: A life-size bronze statue of 'Apoxyomenos' was found as a solitary find. Supposedly a Hellenistic or Roman copy of the famous Greek C4th original. Dated by organic material found inside the statue.

properties.name: La Tradelière
properties.cargo: amphorae; Tens of thousands of hazelnuts, which formed a layer up to a metre thick. Pottery including Pompeian red plates, rimless platters, fine-wall goblets and two-handled and four-handled cups, as

properties.name: Porto Novo
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Over an area of 15m x 7m, four column fragments between 2.2m and 4.8m long with a diameter of 1.5m; five blocks, one of which is large at 23.3 tons, all in an

properties.name: Jakljan Island
properties.cargo: marble; sarcophagi; marble:Three sarcophagi with lids.

properties.name: Punta Mazza
properties.cargo: amphorae; Corinthian relief cups showing the exploits of Heracles, battle scenes, ritual scenes and hunting scenes.

properties.name: Les Riches Dunes 5
properties.cargo: marble; columns; blocks; marble:A partially worked block of Luna marble 2m x 1m x 0.4m = 3.3 tons buried in sediment.Twenty-one plaques of grey marble; all are broken, but polished on both sides,  the

properties.name: Ouest Embiez 1
properties.cargo: amphorae; Principal cargo of colourless glass: irregular blocks of very good quality raw glass up to 25kg each with a total of at least 10 tons stacked in the centre of the boat. More than fifteen fra

properties.name: Porticcio
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Two 2m high marble statues representing the Roman Emperor Philip 1 of Arabia (ruled AD 244-249) and his wife Marcia Otacilia, in fragments but weighing about 600kg ea

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 18
properties.cargo: marble; blocks; marble:Seven blocks of Luna marble in disorder over an area 8m long. The main block measures 2.8m x 1.1m, the others are smaller (1.4m x 1.7m, 1.8m x 1.5m etc).

properties.name: Saintes Maries-de-la-mer 21
properties.cargo: marble; blocks; marble:Seven blocks of Luna marble, two of which are completely buried in sediment. The heaviest load of the three marble wrecks at Saintes Marie.

properties.name: Saintes Maries-de-la-mer 22
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; columns; blocks; marble:Six or seven blocks of Luna marble over an area 12m long, parallel to the beach. Two of the blocks are half cylindrical, i.e. part columns, 2.1m long. Three o

properties.name: Punta del Serrone
properties.cargo: amphorae; A bronze foot (45cm) was found in 1972, followed by another 150 pieces of bronze sculpture in 1992 in an area 15m x 10m. Another foot, two statues, one late Hellenistic male nude, another Au

properties.name: Coscia di Donna
properties.cargo: marble; blocks; marble:A central file of three blocks of white marble 2.2msq.; on each side are four rectangular blocks 2.8m x 1.2m x 0.8m; at the end, four rectangular blocks of the same dimensions.

properties.name: Margarina Point
properties.cargo: marble; columns; blocks; marble:Several unfinished blocks and  20 worked white marble columns.; Flat roof tiles and curved roof tiles.

properties.name: Pakleni/ Izmetište
properties.cargo: amphorae; blocks; marble:About 10 half-finished stone blocks, one of them dark greenish granite, while others are of limestone.; About 2,500 pieces of various  pottery, half are Eastern Sigillata B (p

properties.name: Viganj (Sv. Ivan promontory)
properties.cargo: Estimated 50,000 pieces of Eastern coarse ware pottery, much looted, including bowls, dishes, trefoil mouth jugs, pots, lids, platters with profiled rim, braziers etc. More than 200 pieces were raised

properties.name: Čavlena
properties.cargo: marble; columns; marble:Marble ‘elaborated’ columns and other stones.

properties.name: Cap Camarat 2
properties.cargo: amphorae; Red slip Arretine ware. Eastern sigillata A.

properties.name: Dramont I
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; blocks; marble:In an area of 500m sq, three blocks of africano marble were found (2.2m x 1m x 1m; 3.8m x 0.95m x 0.8m; 2.68m x 1.1m x 1m). A total weight of 23 tons.; A complementary

properties.name: Sanguinaires
properties.cargo: amphorae; Coarse ware, fineware, red and black-glazed pottery. Attic lamps. Small copper ingots and millstones as ballast. At least 550kg of glass, both manufactured and unmanufactured.

properties.name: Grado (Julia Felix)
properties.cargo: amphorae; Bronze stands with lions’ paws and acanthus decoration. Glass, much of it waste glass, packed in a barrel which had disintegrated; some square bottles with the base mark C SALVI GRATI.

properties.name: Torre Chianca
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Five marble columns, 8-9m long of cipollino, lying approximately 50cm apart, still in the original position in which they were transported. Another 2 are still buried

properties.name: Palagruza B (Pupak Reef)
properties.cargo: amphorae; Pottery, numerous sherds of clay mortaria and, uniquely for the Adriatic,  chamber pots (lasanua). South Italic services of 'orlo bifido' dishes with plates/lids and Pompeian red ware plates

properties.name: Supetric
properties.cargo: A large quantity of tegulae and imbrices suggests a shipwreck.

properties.name: Atlit A
properties.cargo: Parker notes Assyrian bronze helmets, Babylonian weights (in the form of swans), furniture and candelabras.

properties.name: Acque Chiare
properties.cargo: amphorae; At least 4 terracotta 'vaulting tubes', but these may not have been part of the cargo.

properties.name: Agay A (Camp Long)
properties.cargo: amphorae; At least 7 basalt grinding stones atnd 250 bronze ingots of slender form, 38-40cm long. A brass ingot and some copper vessels were possibly also part of the cargo.

properties.name: Agde G
properties.cargo: Tablets of litharge (red lead) clay were stacked together in an area 20 x 14m, representing about 100 tons.

properties.name: Agde J
properties.cargo: A group of lead ingots of Republican Spanish type. They bear the mould-marks L. PLANI.L.F.RUSSINI, SOC GARGILI T F ET M LAETILI ML and SOC.M.C. PONTILIENORUM.M.F..The ingot of L. Planius Russinius is

properties.name: Agde K
properties.cargo: A group of lead ingots, cast in oval mounds resembling the "Pinna nobilis" shell and inscribed with TT. Compare the ingots from the Cabrera B wreck.

properties.name: Ajaccio
properties.cargo: Yellow or green glazed ware pottery 'a stecca' (ribbed), imitating products of Pisa, commonly found in Liguria and less frequently in Corsica.

properties.name: Albenga
properties.cargo: amphorae; A variety of pottery: black-gloss ware, including Campanian A plates and 'imitation Campanian' bowls. Some is said to have been stacked between the amphorae.

properties.name: Alcudia
properties.cargo: 'A considerable number' of tin ingots is said to have been found. About 30cm tall, with a hole near the top, and weighing 10.5-11.5 kg. They were slightly alloyed with silver.

properties.name: Antheor
properties.cargo: Glazed ware similar to that of the wreck Ajaccio.

properties.name: Bagaud B
properties.cargo: Tin and iron ingots, some marked. According to one round inscription, the merchants were Celts, specifically Hypocelts who were assembled by a Greek named Heracleides for the coomercial exploration of

properties.name: Preveto
properties.cargo: amphorae; Cups, pitchers and bowls. Many tiles.

properties.name: Calanque de l'Ane
properties.cargo: A cargo of tiles, tegulae and imbrices, still in rows.

properties.name: Camarina B
properties.cargo: A block of 73 North African oil lamps that had been carried in fabric sacks, and all were marked with the maker's mark STACTE as well as being decorated

properties.name: Sud Lavezzi 5
properties.cargo: Tegulae; ceramics, terra sigillata plates from N Italy etc

properties.name: Cape Gelidonya A
properties.cargo: amphorae; 34 flat four-handled copper ingots (25kg each) cast in the shape of an ox hide, several tin ingots, several raw bronze ingots and broken bronze tools (scrap axes, adzes, knives and a spade c

properties.name: Carry-le-Rouet
properties.cargo: blocks; marble:24 tons of local French limestone.

properties.name: Dhokos
properties.cargo: Most of the 1,381 objects recorded were vase fragments dating from the Early Helladic II  period, inlcuding saucers, deep and medium-depth bowls, pots, jugs and plates. Fragments of a pyxis, a cylindr

properties.name: Dramont A
properties.cargo: marble; blocks; marble:Stone blocks measuring 4 x 1 x 1m

properties.name: Dramont C
properties.cargo: amphorae; Iron bars and blocks of resin; 3 millstones. Numerous ceramics, ollae, olpes, jars, plates, cups and some copper tableware.

properties.name: Dramont E
properties.cargo: amphorae; 30 tubuli of which 22 were intact may have been  part of the cargo? 800-900 plates of terra sigillata D  (Hayes 61B, Hayes 64, Hayes 65 and Hayes 50 B), some with geometric motifs, others bo

properties.name: Dramont G
properties.cargo: Tegulae and imbrices from Frejus, cf Bartelemy B and Roches d'Aurelle wrecks.

properties.name: Favaritx
properties.cargo: Hundreds of bronze plates, keys, chains, candelabra, statuettes, balances and coins - Christian liturgical objects,

properties.name: Giens A/Redonne B1
properties.cargo: amphorae; Campanian A ceramics - Lamboglia 27Ba cups with palms et 27c with rosettes as well as small cups L34a.

properties.name: Giglio Campese A
properties.cargo: amphorae; Attic and Corinthian ceramics, circular ingots (80cm diameter).

properties.name: Gusteranski
properties.cargo: amphorae; South Italian Pompeian ware consisting of 'orlo bifido' bowls, with plates/lids and standard Pompeian red plates, which were only 15% of the total of 50 brought to the surface.

properties.name: Isis
properties.cargo: amphorae; Common ware pottery, mostly Tunisian, datable to c AD 400, some are unique in style. A basalt millstone from Libya with a concave hopper and protruding handles is an previoulsy unknown form.

properties.name: Ladispoli A
properties.cargo: amphorae; A wooden head of a duck as decoration for a kline.

properties.name: Les Laurons B
properties.cargo: amphorae; Wheat - grains were preserved in pitch

properties.name: Lavezzi A (1)
properties.cargo: amphorae; At least 7 lead ingots and 18 copper ingots.

properties.name: Ma'agan Mikha'el
properties.cargo: Seventy Cypriot and East Greek clay vessels, including a pithos, basket-handled jars, oil lamps, small jugs,mortaria and a cooking pot. 12 tons of rocks, mainly blueschist from Euboea, as well as some

properties.name: Mal di Ventre
properties.cargo: amphorae; 1100 Spanish lead ingots mined from Cartagena, each weighing about 100 Roman pounds and inscribed on theupper side with the manufacturer's mark. One was SOCMPONTILIENORUMMAE, ie Societas Mar

properties.name: Marzamemi B
properties.cargo: A cargo of pre-fabricated marble architectural pieces from a Byzantine church, including 28 bases and 27 capitals of Proconnesian marble, and an 'ambo' with 2 staircases from which a priest would deli

properties.name: Mateille A
properties.cargo: A bronze consignment of female statue fragments, animal terminals for furniture, boar's tusk decoration, pendants, dishes and pots.

properties.name: Minorca
properties.cargo: amphorae; Bronze ingots weighing 80kg each, still arranged in rows.

properties.name: Plemmirio A
properties.cargo: Bronze lamps, censers, pitchers, bowls and a steelyard.

properties.name: Port-Miou (Port Miou B)
properties.cargo: amphorae; Sigillata D or African redslip, Hayes 81 B and 67 B 33

properties.name: Port Vendres C/ 3
properties.cargo: amphorae; A small amount of glass tableware.

properties.name: Port Vendres E
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; marble:Five unfinished sheets of Luna marble.

properties.name: Porticello
properties.cargo: amphorae; At least two dozen lead ingots, looted, only 2 have been excavated that are stamped, probably from Laurion. Eight small clay inkpots. Bronze sculpture: a superb male bearded  head, approxima

properties.name: Punta del Diavolo
properties.cargo: marble; columns; blocks; marble:A cargo of stone (marble??) comprising of blocks, slabs, small columns and pillars. It is covered in in concretions which hinders exact definition of the material, but

properties.name: Roches d'Aurelle
properties.cargo: amphorae; Tegulae and imbrices from Frejus, cf Bartelemy B and Dramont G wrecks.

properties.name: Saint Gervais B (2)
properties.cargo: amphorae; Wheat - a layer of pitch spilt from the amphorae glued  this rich archaeological layer of organic material  in position in the back one third of the ship.

properties.name: Saint Gervais C
properties.cargo: amphorae; 11 glass ungentaria, 10 of which were found in a basket; an ESA plate;

properties.name: Salakta
properties.cargo: marble; columns; marble:Architrave and pilaster parts.

properties.name: Saturo A
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; marble:Limestone blocks.; Campania A black-gloss pottery.

properties.name: Secca dei Mattoni
properties.cargo: amphorae; Ceramics mainly consist of cups of the Morel 2323 and 1222 type, Lamboglia 3 vases and ciboria (Morel 7450 and 7550 types), other forms such as the Lamboglia 5 (low bowls without handles) sm

properties.name: Serçe Limani A
properties.cargo: Two tons of raw glass and one ton (between half a million and a million sherds) of broken glass (broken prior to loading on board and possibly stored in baskets as they were found in clumps) as well a

properties.name: Sud-Lavezzi B (2)
properties.cargo: amphorae; 95  lead ingots arranged in 11 rows all bearing the the mould-mark MINVCIORVM and the counter stamp AP.IVN and ZETH and graffiti indicating their weight (51-54kg). 237 circular copper ingots

properties.name: Tour du Castellas 1
properties.cargo: amphorae; A bronze Etruscan tripod dated to the beginning of the C5th BC.

properties.name: La Tour Fondue
properties.cargo: amphorae; Massiliot ceramics - cups and millstones.

properties.name: Ulu Burun
properties.cargo: amphorae; Ten tons of Cypriot copper ingots (354 oxhide ingots and 121 smaller bun- and pillow-shaped ingots). Nearly a ton of tin ingots. Raw ivory (elephant tusk and hippo teeth!) and ivory carvings

properties.name: Cala Piomba
properties.cargo: amphorae; Two lead ingots of trapezoidal shape

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 2
properties.cargo: amphorae; Iron ingots and 146 bars some of which are very well preserved with stamps EROTIS and LEPIDI, plus symbols of anchors and other unidentifiable oval shapes. Some measure as long as 123cm and

properties.name: La Giraglia
properties.cargo: amphorae; Six fragments of Aretine ware, one with a stampTHALI. Four fragments of blue-green glass from 2 plates and 2 cups.; amphora_type:Dr. 2-4 Tarraconensis

properties.name: Marsa -El-Brega Byzantine
properties.cargo: amphorae; Fineware - Hayes Late Roman C, Form 3, Type H - cooking pots and a sherd of African redslip ware

properties.name: Point Iria
properties.cargo: amphorae; 25 pieces of Mycenaean and Cypriot pottery, and Cretan transport jars. "Cypriot pithoi and LH/LM IIIB 2 pithoid jars) and medium size ones (Cretan stirrup-jars, Cypriot jugs and an amphora w

properties.name: Tanit
properties.cargo: amphorae; Timber, most probably cedars from Lebanon, bundles of wool and potter's clay.

properties.name: Elissa
properties.cargo: amphorae; Timber, most probably cedars from Lebanon, bundles of wool and potter's clay.

properties.name: Tektaş Burnu
properties.cargo: amphorae; Pottery appears to have been made in Ionia or on Chios, including a modest assortment of 2 handled drinking cups, one-handled bowls, table amphorae, oil lamps, a jug, a water pitcher and an

properties.name: Tantura A/Dor J
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:A fragment of pavement from Trench IV and 2 slabs and 4 small fragments from Trench VI.; Plenty of organic material.

properties.name: Tantura B/Dor N
properties.cargo: Numerous remains of basketry, some of it amazingly delicate. Ceramics. Egyptian??

properties.name: Tantura C
properties.cargo: amphorae; Coarsewares, including perfectly preserved jugs.

properties.name: Les Sorres X
properties.cargo: amphorae; A few fragments of bowls.

properties.name: Le Grand Ribaud F (A??)
properties.cargo: amphorae; Etruscan and Greek black-figure ceramics and bronze drinking bowls.

properties.name: Pointe Lequin 1A
properties.cargo: amphorae; Small bronze statue of a helmeted soldier; a large amount of Attic coarse ware and B2 cups (about 2,500 pieces) - decorated with scenes of  battle, mythology and daily life; oil lamps

properties.name: L'Isolella
properties.cargo: Tegulae (57 complete and 388 fragments) and imbricae (56 complete and 80 fragments).

properties.name: Pigne (Salerno)
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; marble:Over a hundred marble closed pine cones.; Glazed ceramic vessels

properties.name: Ottiolu
properties.cargo: amphorae; marble; columns; marble:Finished grey, fine-grained marble slabs, possibly from Luna.

properties.name: Cala Reale
properties.cargo: amphorae; A shipment of glass paste tesserae accompanied the main cargo of amphorae.

properties.name: L'Asinara 2
properties.cargo: amphorae; A large concretion of minerals or metals.

properties.name: Cala del Falco 1
properties.cargo: amphorae; In the late '70s a 4m long lead bar was still visible .

properties.name: Capo Galera
properties.cargo: amphorae; Glazed pottery - bowls, plates and casseroles

properties.name: Les Mouettes
properties.cargo: amphorae; Tiles, copper ingots and lead ingots some of which are stamped M AURELI; a bronze tripod

properties.name: Les Pierres
properties.cargo: 75 blocks of stone from Bois des Lens, north-west of Nimes, including 7 columns and capitals. The blocks were still lying in alignment and were rectangular; the dimensions varied between 0.7 0.75 x 0.

properties.name: Les Tambours
properties.cargo: columns; marble:Sixteen drums measuring between 0.5 and 0.8m high with traces of inscriptions, possibly from Les Lens

properties.name: Bonnieu 5
properties.cargo: marble; blocks; marble:Three rows of 57 finished limestone blocks

properties.name: Les Riches Dunes 4
properties.cargo: amphorae; Two bronze statues: the first is of a young naked child, 65cm high, possibly Eros, datable to C1st BC- AD. The second is also of a child, in a short tunic, 80cm high and very well preserved,

properties.name: Calanque de Conca
properties.cargo: amphorae; A large amount of fragments of jugs.

properties.name: Marina di Fiori
properties.cargo: amphorae; A secondary cargo of blocks of minerals, never found on a wreck before. Some are of clay and are stamped M.ACILI and VENU

properties.name: Erbalunga
properties.cargo: 32kg of medium and small-sized coins dated to the end of the reign of Constantine I and the end of the C4th AD. The amphora was a Byzantine Keay XXV.

properties.name: Pointe Cacalu
properties.cargo: amphorae; Oil lamps including Dressel-Lamboglia type 3 classic.

properties.name: Les Moines 1
properties.cargo: Some decorated fragments of Arabic ceramics and tableware, some from Pisa

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 6
properties.cargo: Iron ingots and bars, some stamped. Apiece of Campanian pottery was found in one of the concretions dating the wreck to the second half of the C1st BC

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 8
properties.cargo: amphorae; Twenty concretions of iron ingots

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 10
properties.cargo: A concretion 8m long of iron ingots with illegible stamps.

properties.name: Plage d'Arles 5
properties.cargo: Sigillata from La Graufesenque, some stamped, that dates the wreck to the period of Claudius/Vespasian.

properties.name: Sud Caveaux 1
properties.cargo: amphorae; Ceramics found at the far end of the wreck

properties.name: Les Battuts 4
properties.cargo: amphorae; A three-beaked bronze lamp, very rare.

properties.name: Pointe de la Voile
properties.cargo: amphorae; Campanian B ware form L5a

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 4

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 24
properties.cargo: Large iron bars weighing between 23 and 33kg

properties.name: Cavalaire 1
properties.cargo: A huge amount of weapons - at least 8 cannons, projectile weapons, crossbow parts, lance tips

properties.name: Barthelemy B
properties.cargo: Tegulae and imbrices from Frejus cf Dramont G and Roches d'Aurelle wrecks.

properties.name: Lardier 4
properties.cargo: Nine rows of tegulae in 2 layers and imbrices in the residual spaces. A large amount of pottery typical of the lower Argens valley (Rivet form 17)

properties.name: Grand Rouveau 2
properties.cargo: amphorae; Tiles, between 100 and 300 imbrices and probably as many tegulae.

properties.cargo: amphorae; Numerous fragments of unidentified pottery.

properties.name: Punta Licosa 2
properties.cargo: marble; blocks; marble:About 15-20  blocks with dimensions varying from 50 to 100cm which have clearly been squared

properties.name: Capo Donato
properties.cargo: Cooking ware, generally of an open-form type.

properties.name: Capo Colonna, Cicala

properties.cargo: marble; columns; blocks; marble:'Masonry', capitals and columns

properties.name: Tantura E ? Trench IX

properties.name: Herzliya Beach

properties.cargo: amphorae; Ceramics: Ostia III 267 and Ostia I 17 plates

properties.cargo: Decorated bronze handles of large vessels.

properties.name: Nahal Oren
properties.cargo: Bronze furniture/works of art? - bronze attachments of a bed with remains of wood, a stylized bronze handle of a container and  adecorated gold plaque.

properties.name: Mandriola A
properties.cargo: amphorae; Many fragments of African table ware

properties.name: Cala Su Pallosu
properties.cargo: amphorae; Millstones - 4 lower cone-shaped parts (metae) and 5 upper parts (catilli). Glass. Cups.

properties.name: Qefalit Tile wreck
properties.cargo: amphorae; The tile cargo is still stacked and forms an interlocking and compacted deposit. Pan and cover tiles are present of Corinthiam manufacture measuring 29 x 20 inches.

properties.name: La Manga (Bajo de la Campana)
properties.cargo: amphorae; A cargo of ivory, amber and many ceramic objects. The 10 ivory tusks measure between 70 and 150 cm long and are inscribed with Phoenician writing. They have come from a race of elephants now

properties.name: Pseira
properties.cargo: amphorae; Ceramics including jugs, cups and cooking pots.

properties.name: Polyaigos (marble)
properties.cargo: marble; columns; marble:A single column of cipollino from Carystos was found that may come from an otherwise unknown wreck transporting architectural elements.

properties.name: Megalo Stironisi
properties.cargo: A cargo of tiles in sets of 6 and tied in large piles.

properties.name: Cala Sant Vicents 2
properties.cargo: amphorae; Bundles of iron tools, some tin ingots, baskets and grain-rubbers.

properties.name: Boka Kotorska  1
properties.cargo: Pan tiles and cover tiles are in several stacks, many of which are now postitioned vertically. The pan tiles measure c 65 x 45 cm with flanges angled at 90 degrees and are characteristic of Roman manu

properties.name: Bozburun tile wreck
properties.cargo: amphorae; Roof tiles, both cover and pan: over 10 of red fabric with white inclusions have been exposed possibly by an octupus (!). The cover tiles are about 1m long and 0.2-0.3m  wide, and slightly r

properties.name: Les Catalans 2
properties.cargo: marble; columns; marble:The remains of 2 white marble statues.  One of the statues (46cm high) ressembles an Apollo although there is no head, datable to mid C1st AD, whilst the other is simply part o

properties.name: Aci Trezza 2
properties.cargo: A cargo of millstones, of which 20 have been recovered and are now exhibited at Naxos Antiquarium.

properties.name: Isola Rossa 3
properties.cargo: amphorae; A concretion probably of metal

properties.name: Skerki Bank Keith Reef 3
properties.cargo: amphorae; Seven mortaria, a rare find in late Roman wrecks, cf Ustica wreck

properties.name: Skerki Bank Keith Reef 4
properties.cargo: Pieces of raw glass, yellow, green and blue in colour, and weighing between 24 and 126gm. Analysis suggests that are ancient since they consist of sodium silicate which is typical of Roman glass.

properties.name: Skerki Bank Keith Reef 5
properties.cargo: At least 5 lead ingots, weighing between 20 and 25kg, some of which bear stamps.

properties.name: Cape Glavat/Mljet B
properties.cargo: amphorae; Southern Italian pottery, 'orlo bifido' dishes with the plates/ lids (more than 500) and 15 Pompeian red plates. Great quantities of rough glass clumps.

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 9
properties.cargo: Hundreds of iron ingots of differing sizes in 4 rows and at least 2 layers in concreted masses, some stamped. A piece of ceramic?

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 11
properties.cargo: Iron ingots amalgamated with fragments of amphorae.

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-mer 23
properties.cargo: Iron ingots in mass concretions, 3m x 2m

properties.name: Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer 25
properties.cargo: amphorae; About 50 tons of iron bars and ingots in concretion.

properties.name: Camarina bronzes
properties.cargo: amphorae; Bronze objects including oil lamps, bronzes similar to those found in the noble houses of Pompeii and Herculaneum, decorative pots, braziers, chandeliers and parts of statuettes of Mercury.
